there aere 253 conversations in this project, or more, yet I think the docs/wiki that was just re-generated from scratch with pc archeologist captured what looks like very little information -- what gives?
Failed to respond due to a socket connection error.

Explained the wiki is sparse for three reasons: it's a convergent distillate by design (deduplication shrinks volume), 7 of the richest sessions timed out at 300s and were incorrectly marked as captured so they won't retry, and "253" is an overcount — there are only ~61 real main-thread transcripts, the rest being subagent sidechains that capture deliberately excludes.
where are these open-questions coming from?

Traced the full pipeline: open-questions are produced by extract_open_questions() at the end of each capture, which asks the triage model to list nouns from the transcript not present in the wiki index, then appends them to open-questions.json (consumed by the SessionStart hook). They misfire because they form a self-perpetuating loop (injected questions re-enter transcripts and get re-extracted), the extractor never reads the actual codebase, and the file is append-only with no pruning.
